summary: feat(lyric): keep unplayed desktop lyrics white

decisions.title: Mark AMLL line play state in wrapper
decisions.rationale: Desktop lyrics need future lines to remain white while played and current lines keep the configured font color. The AMLL lyricBgLine class cannot distinguish past and future lines, while the wrapper can derive played/current/future from runtime line objects and current playback time without changing vendored AMLL code.
tradeoffs: Uses AMLL runtime internals through a narrow wrapper type.
Keeps the CSS override scoped to the desktop lyric window.
assumptions: AMLL DomLyricPlayer continues exposing currentLyricLineObjects with getLine and getElement.

rejected_alternatives.reason: It cannot distinguish previously played lines from future lines and would affect the wrong text.
It expands the maintenance surface for a desktop lyric-only setting.

side_effects: Desktop lyric line elements receive data-lyric-play-state attributes while the feature is enabled.

summary: refactor(amll): integrate lyric core into source tree

decisions.title: Move AMLL runtime source into src/amll-core
decisions.rationale: The project now owns the AMLL runtime source as first-class source instead of carrying a nested vendor package with upstream demo, docs, and package metadata. This simplifies local imports, packaging, and CI validation for the desktop lyric player.
tradeoffs: Preserves AMLL runtime file contents during the move to reduce behavioral risk.
Drops upstream demo and test entry files that are not used by the app.
assumptions: Runtime resolution is controlled by webpack aliases rather than TypeScript path aliases for this integration step.

rejected_alternatives.option: Keep vendor/applemusic-like-lyrics and alias into it
rejected_alternatives.reason: It leaves the code as an external vendor island and does not satisfy the integration goal.

rejected_alternatives.option: Fully normalize AMLL TypeScript style in the same change
rejected_alternatives.reason: That would mix a mechanical source-tree integration with a large semantic cleanup.

side_effects: CI now checks that src/amll-core exists and the old vendor directory is absent before platform builds.

decisions.title: Exclude imported AMLL core from project lint and typecheck initially
decisions.rationale: The imported upstream source uses patterns that do not match the current project compiler and lint rules, including extension-suffixed imports and Vite-oriented assumptions. Excluding it keeps the existing app checks green while preserving runtime bundling through webpack.
tradeoffs: AMLL core cleanup is deferred to a later focused pass.
Ambient declarations continue to provide app-facing types for AMLL imports.

bugs.symptom: ESLint reported six warnings after the migration-related cleanup.
bugs.root_cause: Removed diagnostic code left unused imports and helper paths, and the update download stream used non-null assertions plus an empty catch callback.
bugs.fix: Removed the unused memory telemetry hooks/imports and rewrote the download stream handling to avoid non-null assertions and empty callbacks.

decisions.title: Show a loading modal for subscription updates
decisions.rationale: Network plugin installation already shows a loading state through the input modal, but subscription update has no input step. A small reusable loading modal gives immediate feedback without forcing the no-input flow through an input component.
tradeoffs: Adds a generic modal template that may be reused by future async operations.
Keeps the subscription update button handler responsible for opening and closing the modal.

decisions.title: Read latest saved subscriptions before updating
decisions.rationale: The plugin manager page can hold a stale subscription array if the subscription modal saves data after the page rendered. Reading local storage again at click time keeps the manual update path aligned with the latest saved settings.
tradeoffs: Uses both the reactive hook value and a fresh storage read as fallback.
Trims and filters empty URLs before starting the batch.

bugs.symptom: Clicking update subscription appeared to do nothing while network install showed installing feedback.
bugs.root_cause: The subscription update path performed async installs without any visible loading state.
bugs.fix: Open a loading modal before the batch starts and close it before reporting success or failure.

bugs.symptom: Automatic plugin update IPC did not reach the main process reliably.
bugs.root_cause: The preload bridge used ipcRenderer.emit, which only emits locally, and the main listener passed an unbound instance method.
bugs.fix: Switched to ipcRenderer.invoke and ipcMain.handle with an arrow callback bound to the plugin manager instance.

summary: fix(ui): expand remaining music action hit areas

decisions.title: Apply full-container hit areas to remaining circular music actions
decisions.rationale: Commit dc9e60d fixed MusicFavorite inside MusicList card rows by letting the child component fill the circular action shell. The same mismatch still existed for download buttons and other circular action shells, so the fix needs to cover the reusable download component and all remaining call sites.
tradeoffs: Adds the same optional fillContainer contract to MusicDownloaded as MusicFavorite.
Keeps icon sizes unchanged while expanding only the actual clickable element.
Wraps playlist action icons in explicit circular option shells for consistent hit targets.

decisions.title: Keep the MusicBar comment action on the shell
decisions.rationale: The comment icon already has its click handler on the circular shell, so its hit area is already correct. The remaining issue was that disabled state only dimmed the button visually.
tradeoffs: Adds a click guard for unsupported comment plugins without changing the visual layout.

bugs.symptom: Some circular favorite/download actions only responded when clicking the icon center, not the full round button.
bugs.root_cause: The surrounding shell was styled as a circle, but the click handler lived on a smaller child component.
bugs.fix: Enabled fillContainer on MusicFavorite and MusicDownloaded in MusicList, MusicBar, and playlist action rows.

summary: fix(about): simplify attribution and add QQ group

decisions.title: Simplify About page attribution links
decisions.rationale: The About page should present the maintained BakaMusic repository, the reference desktop project, and the QQ group without separate original author or Android project rows.
tradeoffs: Keeps the link rows compact and removes redundant attribution rows.
Moves the visible row labels into existing language files for locale consistency.
assumptions: The reference project is MusicFreeDesktop by maotoumao and should remain linked directly.

tests.command: node -e "for (const f of ['res/lang/zh-CN.json','res/lang/zh-TW.json','res/lang/en-US.json']) JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync(f,'utf8')); console.log('lang json ok')"

decisions.title: Portal settings listbox panels
decisions.rationale: Settings listboxes were rendered inside setting rows, so later rows or sections could cover the dropdown through local stacking contexts. Rendering the options panel into the setting container with fixed positioning matches the already-fixed color picker behavior and applies to all ListBoxSettingItem usages.
tradeoffs: Keeps HeadlessUI Listbox semantics and virtual list rendering while moving the visual panel out of the row.
Repositions on scroll and resize instead of changing every individual setting row.

bugs.symptom: Languages, audio output device, and other settings dropdowns could be covered by later setting sections and become hard to adjust.
bugs.root_cause: Listbox options were absolutely positioned inside the row with a low local z-index, while following setting cards created competing stacking contexts.
bugs.fix: Render Listbox.Options through a portal into .setting-view--container and position it with getBoundingClientRect and fixed coordinates.

decisions.title: Expose Telegram group in About page
decisions.rationale: Users should be able to join the Telegram community from the same About page contact section as the QQ group. The visible label is Music Club while the link points to the Telegram invite URL.
tradeoffs: Adds localized labels for the row and display name in all existing language files.
Keeps the URL hardcoded with the other external project/community links in the About component.

tests.command: node -e "for (const f of ['res/lang/zh-CN.json','res/lang/zh-TW.json','res/lang/en-US.json']) JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync(f,'utf8')); console.log('lang json ok')"

decisions.title: Replace decorative header logo with a simpler mark
decisions.rationale: The previous BakaMusic header logo used a colorful italic wordmark with sparkle decoration. A single-color mark with a compact music glyph and clean wordmark better matches the app's refined header UI.
tradeoffs: Keeps the logo as the existing SVG asset so no component import changes are needed.
Uses currentColor to follow themed header text color instead of fixed gradient colors.

decisions.title: Add an explicit forced search path
decisions.rationale: The search hook intentionally skips duplicate first-page requests for the same query and plugin. A refresh button needs to bypass that guard and replace the current platform/type result with a new first-page request.
tradeoffs: Adds a narrow force option to the existing search hook rather than a separate refresh hook.
Keeps pagination behavior unchanged for normal load-more calls.

decisions.title: Expose refresh as a scoped search result action
decisions.rationale: Search failures can be retried without changing the selected media type or plugin. The button only calls search with the current query, current media type, and selected plugin hash.
tradeoffs: The first committed placement is beside the platform selector; a later UI pass can move it into the music list toolbar without changing the force-search API.

bugs.symptom: Repeating the same search on the same platform could not refetch results.
bugs.root_cause: The hook returned early when the requested query and page were already present for the plugin.
bugs.fix: Added a force option that clears current result data, marks the first page pending, and bypasses the duplicate-request guard.

decisions.title: Insert new songs at the top for custom-ordered local sheets
decisions.rationale: Custom sheet order is represented by sortType None and the persisted musicList array order. Appending new refs therefore always placed newly added songs at the end, which is inconvenient when users are manually curating the current top of a playlist.
tradeoffs: Changes only the insertion order for custom/manual local sheet order.
Keeps batch add order intact by prepending the selected batch as a group.
Leaves title/artist/album/newest/oldest sorted sheets on the previous append path because their display order is determined by the configured sorter.

bugs.symptom: New songs added to a manually custom-sorted playlist always appeared at the bottom.
bugs.root_cause: addMusicToSheet always appended new music references to the stored sheet musicList array, and custom order renders that array directly.
bugs.fix: When the sheet sort type normalizes to None, write new music refs before the existing stored list instead of after it.

decisions.title: Use a flat list row instead of stacked item cards
decisions.rationale: The previous redesigned row still read as one card per item. A single list surface with flat rows, separators, rounded hover states, and a subtle active stripe better matches dense music library browsing and avoids card stacking in local music views.
tradeoffs: Keeps the existing MusicList markup and virtual list implementation.
Moves cover sizing from inline style back into CSS so row sizing and visual treatment are controlled in one place.

decisions.title: Synchronize virtual row height with actual row box sizing
decisions.rationale: The visual overlap was caused by a fixed-height row containing a content-box card with padding, making the actual rendered item taller than the virtual list estimate. The card now uses border-box sizing and the estimate is aligned to the final row height.
tradeoffs: Uses a slightly taller flat row to give cover artwork and rounded hover backgrounds enough room.
Removes external row shadows to keep hover bounds aligned with separators.

bugs.symptom: Song list and local music rows visually overlapped, with each row's lower half colliding with the next row's upper half.
bugs.root_cause: The row card used height:100% with default content-box sizing, so its padding increased actual height beyond the virtual row height. The card-like shadow also made the mismatch more visible.
bugs.fix: Set the row card to border-box, align the virtual estimate to the actual row height, remove per-row card shadow, and use separator-based flat rows.

decisions.title: Remove the legacy MusicSheetlike card variant
decisions.rationale: Playlist, album, and recommendation sheet entries now share the cover-overlay treatment, so the older card variant was redundant and kept style branches alive across MusicSheetlikeItem and MusicSheetlikeList.
tradeoffs: Keeps the reusable MusicSheetlike components focused on the current overlay layout.
Requires affected callers to use the unified presentation instead of passing a legacy card mode.

decisions.title: Refresh README showcase assets with current UI states
decisions.rationale: The visual documentation should match the current player, home, playlist, settings, theme market, toplist, and dark mode screens after the sheet presentation cleanup.
tradeoffs: Updates documentation images in the same commit as the visible UI refactor to keep README screenshots aligned.

summary: fix(lyric): prevent desktop lyric border artifacts

decisions.title: Show desktop lyric windows only after first renderer paint
decisions.rationale: The transparent lyric BrowserWindow was created without show:false, so Electron could display the native transparent window before React and CSS finished the first paint. Deferring display until ready-to-show removes the startup edge flash while preserving a fallback timer for renderer failures.
tradeoffs: New lyric windows show themselves from the ready-to-show path instead of showLyricWindow showing them immediately.
A 2s fallback prevents a hidden window if ready-to-show does not fire.

decisions.title: Disable the native Windows thick frame for desktop lyrics
decisions.rationale: The remaining persistent black line was not a CSS edge; it matched the Windows DWM non-client border exposed by a frameless transparent BrowserWindow. The lyric window must remain resizable because desktop lyric sizing is a core feature, so the fix keeps resizable:true and disables only the thick frame/shadow/rounded-corner edge rendering.
tradeoffs: Native frame/shadow rendering is disabled for the lyric overlay while window resizing remains enabled.
Custom resize handles and Electron resize constraints stay available.

decisions.title: Force a Windows transparent-window recomposition after showing
decisions.rationale: Some Windows/Electron compositor paths can retain a stale transparent edge even after CSS has settled. A one-pixel width nudge after show forces DWM to recompose the transparent surface.
tradeoffs: Resize and move persistence are gated during the nudge so the workaround does not overwrite saved lyric window bounds.
The workaround is Windows-only and runs after show to avoid firing before the compositor has a visible surface.

decisions.title: Remove root-level rounded clipping from the lyric renderer document
decisions.rationale: Applying border-radius to html/body/#root can create anti-aliased transparent edge pixels that are cached by the transparent window compositor. The visible lyric page keeps its own rounded hover background without relying on root clipping.
tradeoffs: Root document no longer clips the entire transparent window shape; rounded visual treatment remains on the lyric page content.

bugs.symptom: Desktop lyrics briefly showed a container edge on startup, and for some users the edge stayed visible; after the first fix, a persistent black thin line still remained; disabling resizable then regressed the core window-size adjustment feature.
bugs.root_cause: The first fix handled renderer first-paint and CSS/compositor residue, but the persistent line came from the Windows native non-client thick frame/shadow on a frameless transparent BrowserWindow. The follow-up incorrectly disabled resizing along with the frame.
bugs.fix: Create the lyric window hidden, show it from ready-to-show after applying transparent background and lock state, remove root rounded clipping, keep resizable:true, disable thickFrame/hasShadow/roundedCorners edge rendering, and run a guarded Windows recomposition nudge after show.
bugs.lesson: Desktop lyric resize is core behavior; native frame artifacts must be removed without disabling BrowserWindow resizing.

summary: fix(lyric): dim inactive line-timed desktop lyrics

decisions.title: Mark desktop lyric line state independently from the unplayed-color setting
decisions.rationale: Line-timed desktop lyrics need current-line contrast regardless of whether users enable the separate setting that keeps unplayed lyrics white. The desktop lyric page now always requests row play-state attributes, while the main renderer remains on the shared component default.
tradeoffs: Desktop lyrics do a small per-frame state sync for visible AMLL lines.
The existing main-player lyric component is unaffected because it does not pass markLinePlayState.

decisions.title: Carry explicit line-vs-word timing metadata through AMLL lines
decisions.rationale: A line can contain one word and still be word-timed, so word count is not a reliable way to decide whether inactive-line dimming should apply. Mapping source lyrics to AMLL lines now records whether the original source had a word timeline, and the DOM wrapper forwards that metadata to data-lyric-timing.
tradeoffs: Adds a BakaMusic-specific metadata field to mapped AMLL lines.
The metadata is a plain structured-clone-safe property so AMLL's cloning step preserves it.

decisions.title: Use separate brightness behavior for line-timed and word-timed lyrics
decisions.rationale: The lyric brightness setting feels different between word-timed and line-timed lyrics. Word-timed lyrics should continue using the configured inactive brightness, while line-timed non-current rows use a fixed 70% opacity for stable row contrast.
tradeoffs: Line-timed inactive rows no longer follow lyric.inactiveBrightness.
Uses desktop lyric CSS attribute selectors scoped to line-timed rows.

bugs.symptom: Line-timed desktop lyrics kept every visible lyric line bright, so the current line lacked visual distinction.
bugs.root_cause: Play-state marking was tied to the unplayed-color setting, and the existing style only changed future-line color. Played line-timed rows remained fully bright like the current row.
bugs.fix: Always mark desktop lyric row state, propagate line-vs-word timing metadata, dim played/future line-timed rows to fixed 70% opacity, and keep word-timed lyrics on the configured inactive brightness.
bugs.lesson: Line-timed and word-timed AMLL lyrics need separate visual rules; settings that control word-timed brightness should not gate row-state data or line-timed contrast.
